engineering.translation: The constant renunciate (nitya-sannyāsī) is the one without aversion (dveṣa) and without longing (ākāṅkṣā), free of dualities (nirdvandvaḥ); such a one is easily released from bondage. Krishna's technical definition of the genuine renunciate is operational: not someone who has left the building, but someone who is free of pull-toward and push-away. The engineering analog is precise. The senior who treats the praised launch and the failed launch with the same fundamental engg response — neither inflated by the praise nor flattened by the failure — is the operating nitya-sannyāsī. Free of dveṣa: does not push away the boring incident-response, the unglamorous maintenance work, the underperforming peer's pull request. Free of ākāṅkṣā: does not chase the visible launch, the executive recognition, the marquee project. Free of dualities: does not collapse into a swing on the praise/blame axis.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer reviews two pull requests in the same hour. The first is from a high-visibility product launch on which executives will see the Datadog dashboards next week. The second is a maintenance fix to a dusty ETL script handling end-of-quarter reconciliation that nobody on Slack will mention. She gives both PRs the identical depth of review: same line-by-line attention, same tone in comments, same engagement with edge cases. She does not lean toward the high-visibility one because it is high-visibility (no ākāṅkṣā). She does not skim the dusty one because it is dusty (no dveṣa). Her colleagues observed: when she reviews, PR business-importance is invisible from her audit-tone. This is the operational nitya-sannyāsī — not someone who has stopped reviewing, but someone whose review is uniform across pull and push gradients.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader concludes that the senior should treat all PRs as identically important to the business. The verse names a property of the reviewer, not of the work. Strategic priority is real; the dusty ETL might genuinely deserve fewer engineering hours than the launch. What the verse refuses is the *swing* — the inflation of attention by visibility, the deflation of attention by absence-of-visibility.
engineering.counter_example: When the engineer is genuinely deciding what to work on, the verse's freedom-from-pull-and-push does not erase the need for prioritisation. Strategic judgment about where engineering hours should go is necessary; the senior who has flattened all judgment in the name of equanimity has misread the verse. The freedom is in the response, not in the prioritisation.
engineering.implication: Audit your last ten code reviews. Were the high-visibility ones reviewed differently from the maintenance ones? If yes, the swing is there. The discipline is uniform engineering response across the visibility gradient.
engineering.quotable_line: The senior is the one whose review depth does not change between the launch PR and the dusty maintenance PR.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 5.3 engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored to chapter-5 thesis: equivalence-of-paths-with-karma-yoga-preference, decoupled identity, sama-darśana, internal-bliss-source, brahma-nirvāṇa-as-operational-rung-with-honest-STRETCHED. Cross-references to chapters 2-4 preserved.
